body_txt: Most people are ( usually ) uncomfortable with other people seeing them naked against their will; as such, one way to humiliate someone is to show indecent material of them nonconsensually. Said material might be shown either by accident or out of malice. Regardless, people who discover that there is porn of them being released without their consent will naturally not take it very well. If said material is released by accident, some typical reasons why include stupidity, carelessness, or circumstances outside of the victim's control. Some situations where this can happen include a private sex tape being released to the public by accident, a naked person unknowingly exposing themselves while being livestreamed, or a couple deciding to go at it while a camera is filming them. The malicious variants of this trope involve characters showing photos and/or videos of someone without their consent. Both the perpetrators and the victims of such an act can range in the level of sympathy depending on the context. Examples where the perpetrator is unsympathetic and the victim is sympathetic include money-hungry people who have no qualms about violating someone's consent, exes who are bitter about being dumped, and bullies who post revenge porn out of spite towards someone. Examples of where the sympathetic and unsympathetic parties are switched include victims of cheating who want to get revenge on their unfaithful partner and/or the homewrecker, victims of bullying getting payback on their tormentors , and victims of this trope doing it back to their tormentors . In addition, the advent of photoshop and deepfakes means that someone can become a victim of this trope even if the producer doesn't have actual footage and/or photos of the victim. See also Revenge Porn Blackmail , in which a person threatens someone with this. Compare Naked People Trapped Outside and Snuff Film . If someone uploads porn of themselves and it backfires on them, it's a case of Posting What You Shouldn't . Note: Despite the fact that fictional characters rarely suffer legal consequences for it, in real life, doing this to someone deliberately is a crime known as "revenge porn." Also, like all sex-related tropes, No Real Life Examples, Please!
